user_query:
I'm organizing my inbox for an upcoming seminar on the ethics of gene editing. Please write and run a script that:

1) Scans all messages in input/emails/*.eml, parses headers and body (including stripping HTML when needed), and uses input/filters.yaml to detect and categorize emails related to gene editing. Treat keyword matching as case-insensitive.

2) Produces output/inquiries.csv with the following columns for each email that matches at least one keyword from filters.yaml:
- message_id (from the Message-ID header)
- date (convert the Date header to ISO 8601 in UTC)
- from_name
- from_email
- subject
- categories (comma-separated, unique, alphabetical by category name)
- matched_keywords (comma-separated, unique, alphabetical, lowercase)
- snippet (the first 200 characters of the plain-text body content with HTML tags removed if the email is HTML)

3) Generates a personalized draft reply for each included email using the template in input/reply_template.md. Save each reply to output/replies/<message_id_stripped>.txt, where <message_id_stripped> is the Message-ID with angle brackets removed. Populate the placeholders {{recipient_name}}, {{subject}}, {{categories}}, and {{snippet}} using the parsed values.

4) Writes output/summary.txt with two lines: the total number of emails processed and the number included in the CSV.

Key constraints and checks:
- Do not hardcode keywords or categories; derive them solely from input/filters.yaml.
- If an email contains multiple categories or keywords, include them all (deduplicated and sorted as specified).
- Only include emails that match at least one keyword.
- The script must be runnable from the workspace and create all outputs under the output/ directory.

Inputs are in input/, and the expected outputs are:
- output/inquiries.csv
- output/replies/<message_id_stripped>.txt files
- output/summary.txt

This will help me quickly triage and respond to inquiries related to gene editing ethics and policy. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
